name: Simplified Document Solutions
description_short: Atlanta-based bankruptcy filing service offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings for $249, plus tax preparation and debt relief services for low-to-moderate income individuals.
description_long: Simplified Document Solutions is a legal and financial services firm based in Atlanta, Georgia (100 Hartsfield Center Parkway, Atlanta 30354) that specializes in affordable bankruptcy assistance. The company is listed as a Black-owned business on the Support Black Owned directory and positions itself as an accessible option for consumers facing financial hardship. The firm provides bankruptcy filing services, professional tax preparation, and related debt relief solutions designed for individuals with limited financial resources. Their primary offering is low-cost bankruptcy filings—both Chapter 7 liquidation and Chapter 13 reorganization plans—priced at a flat $249 fee. Beyond bankruptcy, they offer tax preparation services with additional benefits including free bank products, refund advances, and no charges for Georgia state taxes or e-file fees. The company distinguishes itself primarily through pricing accessibility, targeting low and moderate income individuals who might otherwise be unable to afford legal bankruptcy assistance. Their business model emphasizes transparent, low-cost service delivery without hidden fees. As of the available data, the company has no customer reviews on the Support Black Owned platform, making it difficult to assess service quality or client satisfaction. The 404 errors on their 'About Us' and 'Pricing' pages suggest their directory listing may not be fully maintained or up-to-date, though their alternate website (249bankruptcy.com) appears to be their primary digital presence. Consumers should verify current pricing, qualifications, and attorney credentials independently before engaging services.
pros: Flat $249 fee for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing—significantly below typical market rates
No hidden e-file fees or Georgia state tax charges on tax preparation services
Offers complementary bank products and refund advances with tax preparation
Targets underserved population (low and moderate income individuals)
Black-owned business designation may appeal to consumers prioritizing community support
Combines bankruptcy services with tax preparation for comprehensive financial assistance
cons: Zero customer reviews available on Support Black Owned platform—no verified feedback on service quality
Directory listing appears incomplete or poorly maintained (404 errors on About Us and Pricing pages)
No information provided about attorney qualifications, experience, or credentials
Website content lacks detail about Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 eligibility, bankruptcy timeline, or debt discharge coverage
Contact information limited to phone number and alternative website URL—no email or office hours listed
best_for: Low-income individuals seeking Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy with minimal legal fees
Moderate-income earners exploring Chapter 13 reorganization with budget constraints
Consumers in Georgia needing integrated bankruptcy and tax preparation services
Individuals seeking Black-owned financial services providers in the Atlanta area
diagnosis: Best for Georgia residents with low-to-moderate income seeking affordable bankruptcy filing at a $249 flat rate. Primary caveat: lack of verifiable customer reviews, incomplete directory maintenance, and absence of attorney credential information mean consumers should independently verify licensing, experience, and current pricing before retaining services.
